title: P1-10: DKIM best-effort check (INFO, inconclusive framing)
acceptance_criteria: After the existing SPF and DMARC checks, probe 9 DKIM selectors in parallel via the existing getTxtRecords() helper: google, mail, selector1, selector2, default, mandrill, sendgrid, smtp, dkim — each queried as '<selector>._domainkey.<apex>'.
Use Promise.allSettled (not Promise.all) so a DNS error on one selector does not abort the others.
If ANY selector returns a non-empty TXT record array (i.e., DKIM configured), emit NO finding.
If ALL 9 selectors return empty or error, emit severity=INFO, moduleId='P1-10', category='DNS & Email Security', title='DKIM presence could not be confirmed via common selectors'.

The explanation MUST say: 'We checked 9 common DKIM selector names and found no DKIM record. This is inconclusive — custom or provider-specific selectors (e.g., Amazon SES, Postmark, Mailchimp) may be in use. This does not confirm DKIM is absent.'
Impact: 'If DKIM is truly absent, email authentication is incomplete — combine with DMARC p=none (already flagged above) and this means no authentication enforcement.'
DNS errors treated as inconclusive — do not let a lookup failure produce a confident-sounding warning.
No severity recalibrations in this task (SPF ~all stays LOW, DMARC p=none stays LOW — per Gate 1 decision).
Test: one DKIM selector resolves → no finding emitted.
Test: all selectors return empty → INFO finding with 'inconclusive' wording in explanation.
Test: DNS error on all selectors → INFO finding (treated as inconclusive).
Test: DNS error on some, empty on others → INFO finding (no resolved selector).
All existing P1-10 tests (SPF, DMARC) still pass.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
